items.title: How Facebook, Tinder and Airbnb Are Being Used for Sex Trafficking in Colombia
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>On today’s Big Take podcast, Bloomberg’s Natalie Lung and Antonia Mufarech tell Sarah Holder how apps from US-based tech companies are being used for sex trafficking in Colombia — and why it’s so hard for the companies to keep criminals off their platforms https://trib.al/oMLsJfB</article>

items.title: Ontario Premier Ford Says Trump Tariffs Have 'Created Chaos'
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Ontario Premier Doug Ford criticizes US President Donald Trump's tariff plans, saying they have "created chaos." Speaking on "Balance of Power," Ford says Trump will create a recession.

Sign up for the Economics Daily newsletter to discover what's driving the global economy and what it means for policy makers, businesses, investors and you: https://bloom.bg/4535pfS

items.title: US immigration detains Palestinian student activist at Columbia University
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>A Palestinian student activist at Columbia University who led anti-Israel protests last spring was detained by immigration authorities at his home. U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officials entered the university-owned building in Manhattan on Saturday where Mahmoud Khalil lived and took him into custody, according to his attorney Amy Greer, the Associated Press reported. Bloomberg New York Bureau Chief Katia Porzecanski reports</article>

items.title: Trump's climate actions didn't stop with pulling out of the Paris Accord #pubgmobile
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Since taking office in January, President Trump has set in motion a series of sweeping rollbacks on US climate policy — including withdrawing from the Paris Climate Accord on day one.
On this episode of the Big Take podcast, we discuss how Trump's climate policy moves could impact the US and the rest of the world. Listen to the full episode wherever you get your podcasts https://trib.al/QPQwd5Y</article>

items.title: Chipotle Won't Raise Prices as Tariffs Take Effect, CEO Says
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Chipotle Mexican Grill CEO Scott Boatwright talks about how tariffs are impacting the restaurant chain, he says they are now buying avocados from more countries and they're not raising prices during these uncertain times. Boatwright says a new Hot Honey Chicken is starting to appear in stores and they still plan on building more than 300 restaurants this year. He is on "Bloomberg The Close."
--------

items.title: Stock Rout Picks Up Steam With Recession Warnings Blaring
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>The US stock market is experiencing a sharp decline, with the Nasdaq 100 Index and S&P 500 Index on track for their worst day this year. The decline is attributed to warnings from Trump administration officials about the potential for a rough patch in the American economy, as well as rising concerns about a recession and trade wars. Strategists and economists are raising their odds for a US economic downturn, and the market is bracing for a prolonged period of turbulence, with some predicting a potential decline of 8% in the S&P 500. Mike McKee reports on Bloomberg Radio.
--------

items.title: #Trump says he believes Russia's Putin is ready for peace
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>President Donald Trump says it's getting hard to deal with Ukraine.

US and Ukrainian officials will meet in Saudi Arabia this week to discuss the timing and scope of an initial ceasefire with Russia, with multiple points of tension still to be ironed out.</article>

items.title: #Japan is using robots to try and ease the labor crisis #technology #shorts
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Cat-like robots serving food is a common sight at Japan’s largest table service restaurant chain.
The country is turning to these service robots to cope with its chronic labor crunch and the market is expected to triple by 2030, Shery Ahn reports. https://www.bloomberg.com/news/features/2025-03-06/thousands-of-cat-eared-robots-are-waiting-tables-in-japan</article>

items.title: Many challenges ahead for Canada's Mark Carney #politics #canada #shorts
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Mark Carney has won the race to become Canada’s next prime minister.
That puts the former central banker in charge of the country as it tries to fight off a trade war with US
President Donald Trump that could threaten its economic future. 
The ex-Bank of Canada and Bank of England governor won the contest to lead the Liberal Party of Canada with nearly 86% of the vote. The transfer of power from Justin Trudeau to Carney is expected to take place within days, and it’s possible he will call a national election soon after.
Bloomberg's Nicole Sy reports.
Read more: https://trib.al/4qWrT29</article>

items.title: Stagflation Fears | Bloomberg Surveillance | March 10, 2025
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Bloomberg Surveillance hosted by Tom Keene and Paul Sweeney
March 10, 2025  

Featuring:
•  Lindsey Piegza, Chief Economist at Stifel Financial, reacts to recent and forthcoming economic data, and discusses how tariffs could potentially affect US growth, including the odds of stagflation; President Donald Trump’s protectionist policies and cuts to the federal workforce have dented confidence in US economic outperformance

•  Henrietta Treyz, Director of Economic Policy Research at Veda Partners, examines the odds of a government shutdown at week's end and President Trump's continued rhetoric surrounding tariffs; House Republicans announced a spending bill to keep government agencies open through September 30

•   Derek DeCloet, Executive Editor with Bloomberg News, reports on Mark Carney being poised to succeed Justin Trudeau Canada's prime minister, and the deterioration of US-Canada relations; Carney, a former central banker, has pledged to protect Canada's economy and sovereignty, promising to keep retaliatory tariffs on American goods until the US shows respect and makes credible commitments to free and fair trade

•  Kristen Bitterly, Head of Capital Markets, Americas at Citi Private Bank, explains why it's important for investors to remain constructive in the midst of what she calls an "orderly" market selloff
--------
